title: 8. Writing Test case using Robot file

content.text: Step 1: Create Module folder inside TestSuites folder

content.local_path: _images/8. Writing Test case using Robot file/image-2023-3-28_11-18-16.png

content.text: Step 2: Create a .robot file with the module name.

content.title: Robot framework Syntax
content.content: Check this link for Robot file syntax Robot Framework User Guide

content.text: Step 3: Create an Test Suite. In our example, we are creating a file for LCM called UTFTestLCM.robot which is an Test Suite

content.local_path: _images/8. Writing Test case using Robot file/image-2023-3-28_14-2-45.png

content.text: Test Suite will have different sections

items: Settings - Importing test library, documenation, resource file and variable file
Keywords - creating keywords from existing lower-level keywords
Variables - Defining variables that can be used elsewhere in the test data.
Test Cases - Creating test case from available keywords.
Comments - Additional comments or data. Ignored by Robot Framework.

content.text: First we need write the settings of a test suite.

items: Documentation - Proper name of the Test suite
Suite Setup - Initialization for the test suite → Inside initialize launh the dlt connector as shown in example
Test setup is something that is executed before a test case, and a Test teardown is executed after a test case

content.local_path: _images/8. Writing Test case using Robot file/image-2023-3-28_15-0-4.png

content.text: Step 4: Write test Cases inside test suite
Test cases shall be written inside *** Test Cases *** Section.
In our example, we have two test cases 1. Test PSC Shutdown 2. Test PSC SOH

content.local_path: _images/8. Writing Test case using Robot file/image-2023-3-28_15-10-17.png

content.title: Space between parameters
content.content: For the parameters we need to double space. Wasted two days without knowing this

content.text: Step 5: Example of defining an variable and using it in Test cases
